title: Goonland Civil War
subtitle: Part of the Wars in Goonland (2018–present)

sections.title: Background
sections.content:
Goonland was historically a monarchy ruled by Li Jinduo, whose dynasty held centralised authority over the nation's territory and political institutions for decades. Li Jinduo's reign, later referred to by Royalist sources as the "Golden Goon Era," maintained stability through a combination of hereditary legitimacy and patronage networks.

Following Li Jinduo's overthrow in the late 2010s, Muhammad Adam Shahwi seized power and proclaimed the Islamic Republic of Goonlandistan, establishing a new theocratic-republican order centred on Islamic governance principles. The transition was not peaceful, and Shahwi's consolidation of power alienated significant portions of the population who favoured either democratic reform or the restoration of the old monarchy.

Rising ideological, religious, and political tensions across Goonland's diverse regions ultimately proved irreconcilable through negotiation. The involvement of foreign powers — each pursuing their own strategic interests — transformed domestic grievances into a full-scale multi-faction civil war by 2018.

sections.title: Prelude and Causes
sections.content:
The civil war's origins lie in four distinct and incompatible visions for Goonland's future, each backed by organised factions and external patrons.

The Islamic Republic of Goonlandistan, under Shahwi, claimed the mantle of legitimate central government and received material and diplomatic support from all Middle Eastern states, who viewed a stable Islamic republic on Goonland's soil as strategically preferable to either democratic liberalisation or monarchical restoration.

The Free State of Goonlandia emerged from pro-democracy movements that had long agitated for representative governance. Led by Yeow Ivan, the Free State positioned itself as the internationally progressive alternative to Shahwi's theocratic order. The United States and Israel provided financial, military, and intelligence support, motivated by both democratic ideological alignment and broader regional strategic calculations.

The Lohbites — followers of the charismatic religious leader Lohbin — pursued an alternative theocratic vision: a church state modelled on evangelical-charismatic principles, backed by the extensive Kingdom City Church coalition. This coalition spans fourteen nations including Malaysia, Australia, Cambodia, Singapore, Botswana, the UAE, the United Kingdom, Indonesia, Zambia, Sri Lanka, India, South Africa, New Zealand, and Mexico, giving the Lohbites a uniquely transnational support base.

The Royalists, organised around the exiled Li Jinduo — known among supporters by his self-styled title Supreme Masterbaiter — sought to reconquer former royal territories and restore the monarchy. Crucially, the Royalists secured an unconventional but operationally significant alliance with the Nanjing Honey Select Modding Guild, led by Zirui, whose organisational networks and material contributions provided the faction with unexpected resilience.

sections.title: Combatants and Belligerents
sections.content:
The Goonland Civil War is defined by the simultaneous participation of four distinct armed factions, each with incompatible objectives.

The Islamic Republic of Goonlandistan, led by Muhammad Adam Shahwi, functions as the de facto central government and controls the majority of major urban centres. Its stated strategy is the defence of central authority, the suppression of all rival factions, and the preservation of the Islamic Republican constitutional order. It draws on the broadest foreign support base by virtue of its Middle Eastern backers.

The Free State of Goonlandia, under Yeow Ivan, controls significant portions of Goonland's northern territories and pursues a dual strategy of military defence and international political legitimation. The Free State maintains the most active diplomatic presence of any faction, leveraging its American and Israeli backing to seek recognition in international forums.

The Lohbites, followers of Lohbin, operate primarily in central and southern Goonland and have demonstrated a particular capacity for rapid territorial expansion in areas where the Islamic Republic's control is weakest. Their strategy centres on winning civilian hearts through religious services and charitable operations before consolidating military control. The breadth of the Kingdom City Church coalition gives them unparalleled logistical reach.

The Royalists, under Li Jinduo, represent the smallest faction by conventional military measure but benefit from the organisational ingenuity of Zirui and the Nanjing Honey Select modders. Their strategy emphasises exploiting divisions between the other three factions and targeting symbolic former royal sites to generate propaganda value and recruit nostalgic supporters.

subsections.title: Northern Front: Goonlandistan vs. Free State of Goonlandia
subsections.content:
The northern theatre has been the most conventionally contested front of the war. Goonlandistan forces have repeatedly attempted to dislodge the Free State from its northern territorial base, while Free State commanders have used the terrain and their American-supplied materiel to mount effective defensive operations.

The front has largely stabilised into a grinding positional conflict punctuated by periodic offensives. Neither side has achieved a decisive breakthrough since the initial phase of the war.

subsections.title: Central and Southern Front: Lohbites vs. Islamic Republic and Royalists
subsections.content:
The central and southern regions have seen the most fluid fighting, with Lohbite forces making sustained gains against both Goonlandistan and Royalist positions. Lohbin's use of religious networks for logistics and recruitment has proven highly effective in rural areas where state authority was always thin.

Royalist forces under Li Jinduo have pursued opportunistic campaigns in this theatre, targeting cities with symbolic royal heritage. The Nanjing Honey Select modders' contributions — described in Royalist communiqués in deliberately vague terms — have provided the faction with capabilities that analysts have struggled to categorise within traditional military frameworks.

subsections.title: Foreign Interventions
subsections.content:
Foreign intervention has been a defining feature of the Goonland Civil War from its outset and has consistently prevented any single faction from achieving decisive victory.

Middle Eastern states backing Goonlandistan have provided financial transfers, weapons shipments, and diplomatic blocking of international resolutions that might otherwise have constrained Shahwi's operations. The United States and Israel have supplied the Free State with advanced weaponry, training, and intelligence-sharing arrangements. The Kingdom City Church coalition's support for the Lohbites is primarily logistical and financial, channelled through religious charity networks that are difficult to distinguish from military supply chains. The Nanjing Honey Select modders' support for the Royalists remains the most opaque intervention; its precise nature is disputed in the academic literature.

The overlapping and often contradictory interests of foreign backers have created a situation in which no external power is willing to allow its preferred faction to be defeated, ensuring the war's continuation.

sections.title: Aftermath and Current Status
sections.content:
As of 2026, the Goonland Civil War remains active with no credible peace process underway. All four factions continue to hold territory, and none has demonstrated the military capacity to achieve total victory against the combined resistance of the others.

Civilian suffering has been extensive. The multi-front nature of the conflict means that no region of Goonland has been entirely spared from fighting, displacement, or economic disruption. Humanitarian organisations have reported acute shortages of basic goods in contested zones, and the destruction of infrastructure has severely impaired civilian life across all theatres.

The war's outcome remains genuinely uncertain. The Free State of Goonlandia retains the strongest international legitimacy. Goonlandistan holds the most territory. The Lohbites are expanding fastest. The Royalists, though smallest, have proven more durable than initial assessments suggested. Each faction's survival depends substantially on continued foreign support — meaning that the war's resolution, if it comes, is as likely to be negotiated in foreign capitals as on Goonland's battlefields.
